Science Club Introduction, Objectives and Activities Chunk ID: msajce_clubssocieties_chunk_03 URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/clubssocieties.php#tab3 ~430 tokens Context: Describes the MSAJCE Science Club its purpose of inviting scientists, three sections (Physics, Chemistry, General), guiding principles, and planned activities including experiments and workshops. MSAJCE Science Club The Science Club was started with an aim to invite eminent scientists who would share their experience with students and motivate them to gain scientific knowledge. Activities are designed primarily to introduce students to undertake interesting and exciting science and technology projects. The Science Club consists of a group of students who want to explore the many branches of engineering, mathematics, and science. The club is open to all students. The purpose of the Science Club is to promote interest, understanding, and knowledge of the scientific world amongst college students and the local community. Science Club is one of the largest clubs in the college. The club is divided into three sections: Physics, Chemistry, and General. The club aims to arouse engineering students' interest in learning science, promoting academic achievement in Science subjects. It also helps students to learn more about science outside the classroom and teaches how to apply scientific knowledge in daily life through activities. The club also guides students to develop an independent, logical and creative mind. Activities include experiments in: making electronic components, making invisible ink, electroplating, biodiesels, fire writing, and making venation of leaves. Information about the science club and day-to-day updates are displayed on the notice board. Objectives of Science Club: The five Guiding Principles govern how activities have been developed. Investigator activity offers students the opportunity to: 1. Solve a relevant, science-based problem set within a scenario. 2. Work in pairs or small groups or independently. 3. Take part in practical, hands-on science activities. 4. Think and talk about science during the activity and when sharing results. 5. Share results using a variety of media. MSAJCE Tamil Mandram (Tamil Language and Culture Club) The Tamil Mandram was formed to bring about a wonderful transformation in the youth to keep the Tamil language alive and thriving. The motto of the Mandram is: "Thithikkum then tamil mozhi endrum vaazha vendum! Engum vella vendum!" (Translation: "The sweet honey Tamil language must live forever! Must win everywhere!") The Mandram works to revive and reconstruct the ancient Tamil culture and the identities left behind by ancestors. The vision is for the youth to take pride in their language and move from just speaking Tamil to actively bringing glory to Tamil through action. "Vaazha Tamil! Velga Tamil!" (Translation: "Long live Tamil! Victory to Tamil!") Objectives of Tamil Mandram: 1. To develop Tamil enthusiasm and knowledge among students this is the primary objective of the Tamil Mandram. 2. To spread ancient Tamil culture and heritage among today's youth. 3. To develop technical knowledge in Tamil. 4. To continuously conduct Tamil events and social activities suited to the times. Events Conducted by Tamil Mandram: 1. Irumugam Oru Agam (Two faces, one heart elocution/drama) 2. Pessum Padam (Speaking Picture storytelling) 3. Aadu Puli (Tiger and Goat traditional game) 4. Kaivanna Kaviyam (Colourful Poetry art and poetry) 5. Tent Kottai (Tent Camp cultural event) 6. Vidugadhai Vilaiyattu (Riddle Games)

MSAJCE Tamil Mandram (Tamil Language and Culture Club) The Tamil Mandram was formed to bring about a wonderful transformation in the youth to keep the Tamil language alive and thriving. The motto of the Mandram is: "Thithikkum then tamil mozhi endrum vaazha vendum! Engum vella vendum!" (Translation: "The sweet honey Tamil language must live forever! Must win everywhere!") The Mandram works to revive and reconstruct the ancient Tamil culture and the identities left behind by ancestors. The vision is for the youth to take pride in their language and move from just speaking Tamil to actively bringing glory to Tamil through action. "Vaazha Tamil! Velga Tamil!" (Translation: "Long live Tamil! Victory to Tamil!") Objectives of Tamil Mandram: 1. To develop Tamil enthusiasm and knowledge among students this is the primary objective of the Tamil Mandram. 2. To spread ancient Tamil culture and heritage among today's youth. 3. To develop technical knowledge in Tamil. 4. To continuously conduct Tamil events and social activities suited to the times. Events Conducted by Tamil Mandram: 1. Irumugam Oru Agam (Two faces, one heart elocution/drama) 2. Pessum Padam (Speaking Picture storytelling) 3. Aadu Puli (Tiger and Goat traditional game) 4. Kaivanna Kaviyam (Colourful Poetry art and poetry) 5. Tent Kottai (Tent Camp cultural event) 6. Vidugadhai Vilaiyattu (Riddle Games)

CSBS Department Overview, Key Features & Programme Details Chunk ID: msajce_csbs_chunk_01 URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/csbs.php#tab1 ~440 tokens Context: CSBS overview, TCS industry collaboration, key features, programme details, seats, eligibility and benefits over CSE. B.Tech in Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) is a specialized undergraduate engineering program designed to blend core computer science education with insights into business systems and management. Introduced in collaboration with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to bridge the gap between traditional computer science education and the dynamic needs of the IT and business world in the era of Business 4.0. Curriculum covers: Analytics, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Business Analytics, Financial Management, Economics, Internet of Things, Design Thinking, humanities and value-added science. Key Features: (cid:127) Balanced Curriculum Core CS (Data Structures, Algorithms, OS, DBMS, AI/ML) + Business subjects (Economics, Financial Management, Business Process Management). (cid:127) Industry Collaboration Designed in partnership with TCS; emphasis on employability and industry readiness. (cid:127) Focus Areas Problem-solving using computational thinking, Business analytics, IT systems for business applications. (cid:127) Soft Skills Training Communication, Teamwork, Entrepreneurial thinking. Benefits Over Regular CSE: More interdisciplinary exposure; better suited for tech-enabled business functions; greater alignment with industry expectations in IT services and consulting. B.Tech in Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) is a specialized undergraduate engineering program designed to blend core computer science education with insights into business systems and management. Introduced in collaboration with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to bridge the gap between traditional computer science education and the dynamic needs of the IT and business world in the era of Business 4.0. Curriculum covers: Analytics, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Business Analytics, Financial Management, Economics, Internet of Things, Design Thinking, humanities and value-added science. Key Features: (cid:127) Balanced Curriculum Core CS (Data Structures, Algorithms, OS, DBMS, AI/ML) + Business subjects (Economics, Financial Management, Business Process Management). (cid:127) Industry Collaboration Designed in partnership with TCS; emphasis on employability and industry readiness. (cid:127) Focus Areas Problem-solving using computational thinking, Business analytics, IT systems for business applications. (cid:127) Soft Skills Training Communication, Teamwork, Entrepreneurial thinking. Benefits Over Regular CSE: More interdisciplinary exposure; better suited for tech-enabled business functions; greater alignment with industry expectations in IT services and consulting. Department of Computer Science and Engineering MSAJCE Computer Science and Engineering is one of the popular courses among engineering students. It mainly focuses on programming, computation, analysis of algorithm, program design and testing, software engineering etc for design, development and analysis of software and hardware used to solve the problem of various businesses, scientific and social issues. CSE integrates both computer hardware and software and is intended to develop solid understanding of the entire computer system and its accessories and software. It provides sophistication to the world and touches human lives in all aspects such as education, transportation, entertainment, banking, social media, healthcare and supply chain management. CSE is an interdisciplinary subject linked with psychology, geology science, maths, art, music, bio informatics, electronics, and operation research. CSE: PEOs, PSOs, and all 12 Programme Outcomes as defined by MSAJCE CSE department. Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs) PEO1: Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing the software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. PEO2: Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3: Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs) Engineering Graduates will be able to: PSO1: Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies / tools for real time applications of Industry. PSO2: Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. Programme Outcomes (POs): PO1. Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2. Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3. Design/Development Of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.
